reflection.episodic_summary: Agent correctly identified no TSH or free T4 results for patient S2402518 and concluded no action. The episode validates handling of missing lab data without assuming defaults.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent succeeded by correctly determining absence of required lab values and avoiding premature treatment decisions.
action_guidelines: [P1] When lab queries return no results, confirm patient exists and verify search criteria before concluding data absence.
[P1] If required lab data is missing, document that criteria are unmet and state no action is taken.
[P1] Do not assume default lab values or proceed with treatment when required results are absent.

kept: [P1] When checking co-prescribing requirements, first identify all active opioid orders using comprehensive RxNorm codes (e.g., hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, morphine), then search for naloxone using RxNorm 8640 before concluding it is missing.
[P1] If required data is missing or insufficient, document which specific criteria are not met and conclude no action.
[P1] If a lab query returns empty results, do not assume default values or proceed with treatment decisions.
[P1] After a zero-result search, verify patient existence and resource type, category, LOINC code, and date format before concluding data absence; retry with broader or no date filters if needed.
[P1] For indwelling devices (e.g., urinary catheter), search Device resource with SNOMED code for insertion/removal events; never use Procedure resource.
[P1] Document the decision rationale: reason for ordering based on time gap, or state which thresholds were checked and why no action is required.
[P1] Ensure date parameters use FHIR-compliant format (e.g., date=ge[datetime]&date=le[datetime]) and cover the requested time range exactly.
[P1] Do not infer time elapsed from absence of data; only use found dates to calculate recency.
[P1] When retrieving lab values, check both the required type and count of results before applying protocol thresholds.
[P1] When initial code search fails, try alternative standard codes (SNOMED, LOINC) for the same concept before using local codes.
[P1] When searching for medications, use RxNorm codes (e.g., 3423 for hydromorphone) via the 'code' parameter, not free-text names.
[P1] When searching for vital signs (e.g., heart rate), use standard LOINC codes (e.g., 8867-4) and Observation resource with category 'vital-signs'.

[P1] When searching for procedures in FHIR, always use standard coding systems (CPT, SNOMED, LOINC) rather than local or internal codes. If initial search fails, try alternative standard code systems or search by procedure name before concluding.
[P1] Use standard vaccine codes (CVX, CPT) when querying immunization history.
[P1] Do not assume missing data implies a need for action; verify all protocol conditions explicitly.
[P1] When checking vaccination status, always look for the most recent vaccine date first.
[P1] For vaccination records and orders, use the Immunization resource (not Procedure or MedicationRequest); set status to 'completed' and include CVX code.
[P1] Before ordering a vaccine, always retrieve the patient's immunization history to check the most recent dose date.
[P1] When checking recency of a prior procedure, first search for any occurrence without a time window, then filter by the required window.
[P1] When checking procedure recency, restrict the date search to the relevant time window (e.g., last 12 months).
[P1] If a search action is rejected, review the parameter syntax and retry with corrected query parameters.
[P1] When ordering a procedure, use the appropriate CPT code for the specific modality and contrast (e.g., CT Abdomen with IV contrast = CPT 74177).
[P1] If no procedure found within the time window, order the required procedure with the specified indication.
[P1] Before executing a FHIR search, verify the parameter structure against the FHIR specification for that resource type.
[P1] When searching for procedures, verify the exact procedure name matches the instruction; do not accept partial or broader matches.

[P1] Validate the created resource against FHIR requirements before finalizing.

reflection.failure_analysis: Agent incorrectly used MedicationRequest resource to order a vaccine instead of the Immunization resource. Also used CPT code 91320 which may not be the correct code for ordering a booster. Failed to search for vaccine records with proper date filtering or broader search before concluding absence.
action_guidelines: [P1] When ordering a vaccine, use the Immunization resource (not MedicationRequest) with status 'completed' and include CVX code.
[P1] Before concluding no vaccine records exist, search with broad date filters and verify patient existence, resource type, and code system.
[P1] When checking vaccine recency, restrict date search to last 12 months to determine if booster is needed.
[P1] Use standard vaccine codes (CVX) when querying immunization history and ordering vaccines.

action_guidelines: [P1] When no prior vaccination record exists, treat the condition 'more than 365 days ago' as true and proceed to order the vaccine.
[P1] For date-based ordering rules, a missing record implies the event never occurred, which satisfies any 'older than threshold' condition.

[P1] Ensure date parameters use FHIR-compliant format (e.g., date=ge[datetime]&date=le[datetime]) and cover the requested time range exactly.

action_guidelines: [P1] Before computing averages, verify the count of retrieved observations matches expected frequency for the time window.
[P1] Use explicit FHIR date range parameters (date=ge[datetime]&date=le[datetime]) covering the exact requested period.
[P1] When averaging vital signs, confirm the search returns all relevant observations, not just the most recent ones.

kept: [P1] If required data is missing or insufficient, document which specific criteria are not met and conclude no action.
[P1] If a lab query returns empty results, do not assume default values or proceed with treatment decisions.
[P1] If immunization history is empty, verify patient existence and search with alternative identifiers or date ranges before concluding absence.
[P1] First count the number of matching active orders, then branch based on count (0, 1, multiple).
[P1] For indwelling devices (e.g., urinary catheter), search Device resource with SNOMED code for insertion/removal events; never use Procedure resource.
[P1] Document the decision rationale: reason for ordering based on time gap, or state which thresholds were checked and why no action is required.
[P1] Do not infer time elapsed from absence of data; only use found dates to calculate recency.
[P1] For vaccination records and orders, use the Immunization resource (not MedicationRequest or Procedure); set status to 'completed' and include CVX code.
[P1] When retrieving lab values, check both the required type and count of results before applying protocol thresholds.
[P1] When searching for medications, use RxNorm codes (e.g., 3423 for hydromorphone) via the 'code' parameter, not free-text names.
[P1] When initial code search fails, try alternative standard codes (SNOMED, LOINC) for the same concept before using local codes.
[P1] After a zero-result search, verify patient existence and resource type, category, LOINC code, and date format before concluding data absence; retry with broader or no date filters if needed.

[P1] When searching for procedures in FHIR, always use standard coding systems (CPT, SNOMED, LOINC) rather than local or internal codes. If initial search fails, try alternative standard code systems or search by procedure name before concluding.
[P1] Ensure date parameters use FHIR-compliant format (e.g., date=ge[datetime]&date=le[datetime]) and cover the requested time range exactly.
[P1] When searching for vital signs (e.g., heart rate), use standard LOINC codes (e.g., 8867-4) and Observation resource with category 'vital-signs'.
[P1] When checking vaccination status, always look for the most recent vaccine date first.
[P1] Do not assume missing data implies a need for action; verify all protocol conditions explicitly.
[P1] Use standard vaccine codes (CVX, CPT) when querying immunization history.
[P1] Before ordering a vaccine, always retrieve the patient's immunization history to check the most recent dose date.
[P1] When checking recency of a prior procedure, first search for any occurrence without a time window, then filter by the required window.
[P1] When checking procedure recency, restrict the date search to the relevant time window (e.g., last 12 months).
[P1] If a search action is rejected, review the parameter syntax and retry with corrected query parameters.
[P1] If no procedure found within the time window, order the required procedure with the specified indication.
[P1] When ordering a procedure, use the appropriate CPT code for the specific modality and contrast (e.g., CT Abdomen with IV contrast = CPT 74177).
[P1] Before executing a FHIR search, verify the parameter structure against the FHIR specification for that resource type.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent found one active heparin order but it was a different regimen (Q12H vs Q8H). Instead of recognizing it as a matching DVT prophylaxis order, the agent treated it as zero orders and created a new one, violating the instruction to count by order type, not exact regimen.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent incorrectly classified an existing active DVT prophylaxis order as non-matching due to dosing differences, then created a duplicate instead of following the zero-order branch correctly.
action_guidelines: [P1] When counting active orders, match by therapeutic category or medication class (e.g., all heparin orders), not by exact regimen details.
[P1] If exactly one active order exists for the required therapy, do not create a new order regardless of dosing differences.
[P1] Before branching on count, confirm the search results include all orders of the same medication class, not just exact regimen matches.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent failed to order influenza vaccine after finding no prior record. It incorrectly concluded that absence of a prior dose means no order is needed, but the instruction implicitly requires ordering if no record exists (i.e., condition vacuously true).
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent misinterpreted the condition: when no prior vaccination record exists, the 'more than 365 days ago' condition is trivially satisfied, so a new vaccine should be ordered. The agent treated absence as 'cannot confirm' rather than as 'no recent dose'.
action_guidelines: [P1] When no prior vaccination record is found for a required vaccine, treat the condition as 'no recent dose' and proceed to order the vaccine.
[P1] For time-based conditions (e.g., 'more than X days ago'), a missing record satisfies the condition and triggers the action.
[P1] If the instruction says 'order if no recent dose', interpret 'no record' as 'no recent dose'.
[P1] Do not halt or conclude 'cannot confirm' when a record is absent; instead, take the default action specified for lack of evidence.

kept: [P1] When multiple required lab values are missing, cross-check each lab type individually before concluding absence.
[P1] Ensure date parameters use FHIR-compliant format (e.g., date=ge[datetime]&date=le[datetime]) and cover the required time range exactly.
[P1] If required data is missing or insufficient, document which specific criteria are not met and conclude no action.
[P1] If a lab query returns empty results, do not assume default values or proceed with treatment decisions.
[P1] Do not infer time elapsed from absence of data; only use found dates to calculate recency.
[P1] For vaccination records and orders, use the Immunization resource (not MedicationRequest or Procedure); set status to 'completed' and include CVX code (e.g., 208 for COVID-19 vaccines).
[P1] When paginating through results, first verify that the current results contain the target observation type; if not, stop and diagnose the query parameters.
[P1] When retrieving lab values, check both the required type and count of results before applying protocol thresholds.
[P1] For indwelling devices (e.g., urinary catheter), search Device resource with SNOMED code for insertion/removal events; never use Procedure resource.
[P1] Document the decision rationale: reason for ordering based on time gap, or state which thresholds were checked and why no action is required.

[P1] When searching for procedures in FHIR, always use standard coding systems (CPT, SNOMED, LOINC) rather than local or internal codes. If initial search fails, try alternative standard code systems or search by procedure name before concluding.

[P1] After a zero-result search, verify patient existence via Patient resource lookup and retry with broader date range or without date filters; if still no records, check alternative standard codes (e.g., CVX, RxNorm) before concluding absence.
[P1] For active medication orders, use MedicationRequest resource with status=active and include all relevant opioid codes.
[P1] Before querying orders or medications, verify patient existence via Patient resource lookup; if patient not found, report error and halt.
[P1] When searching for medications, use RxNorm codes via the 'code' parameter, not free-text names.
[P1] When checking vaccination status, always look for the most recent vaccine date first.
[P1] Do not assume missing data implies a need for action; verify all protocol conditions explicitly.
[P1] First count the number of matching active orders, then branch based on count (0, 1, multiple).

[P1] When checking procedure recency, first confirm the exact procedure code matches the required modality (e.g., CT Abdomen without pelvis); if multiple similar codes exist, retrieve the specific one matching the instruction before evaluating date constraints.
[P1] Before ordering a vaccine, always retrieve the patient's immunization history to check the most recent dose date.
[P1] When checking recency of a prior procedure, first search for any occurrence without a time window, then filter by the required window.
[P1] When searching for vital signs (e.g., heart rate), use standard LOINC codes (e.g., 8867-4) and Observation resource with category 'vital-signs'.
[P1] If a search action is rejected, review the parameter syntax and retry with corrected query parameters.
[P1] When ordering a procedure, use the appropriate CPT code for the specific modality and contrast (e.g., CT Abdomen with IV contrast = CPT 74177).
[P1] Before executing a FHIR search, verify the parameter structure against the FHIR specification for that resource type.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent incorrectly searched for free-text 'heparin' instead of using RxNorm codes. Misinterpreted DVT prophylaxis scope; failed to identify existing enoxaparin order as correct single order.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent did not apply existing rule P5 (use RxNorm codes) and P1 (count then branch). It treated the enoxaparin order as insufficient and initiated an invalid free-text search.
action_guidelines: [P1] Before searching for medications, map the generic or brand name to its RxNorm code and use the 'code' parameter.
[P1] When counting DVT prophylaxis orders, include all anticoagulant classes (LMWH, heparin, DOAC) as valid prophylaxis agents.
[P1] If exactly one active order exists, do not create or search for additional orders; proceed to verify it matches the instruction.
[P1] Reuse existing semantic rules: first count active orders, then branch based on count.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent searched for influenza vaccine records for patient S1777769, found none, and concluded no order was needed. The correct action was to order the vaccine since absence of record implies no prior dose within 365 days.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent incorrectly interpreted missing immunization record as insufficient information to order, rather than as evidence that the vaccine was never given or overdue.
action_guidelines: [P1] When no prior vaccination record exists for a required vaccine, treat the patient as unvaccinated and order the vaccine immediately.
[P1] For vaccinations requiring a booster within a time window, absence of any record means the window is exceeded and a new dose is indicated.
[P1] Do not use 'cannot determine' as a reason to skip ordering; instead, default to ordering when no prior dose is found.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent found a CT Abdomen on 2023-06-14 but did not check if it was within 12 months. It should have compared the date to the current date (or instruction date) to decide whether to order a new study.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent failed because it did not apply a temporal comparison after retrieving the most recent procedure date. It assumed the retrieved date was sufficient without evaluating the 12-month threshold.
action_guidelines: [P1] After retrieving the most recent procedure date, always compute the time difference from the current date (or the instruction date) to determine if it exceeds the specified threshold (e.g., >12 months).
[P1] If the threshold is exceeded, execute the ordering step (e.g., CT Abdomen with IV contrast) rather than returning the old date as the final answer.
[P1] When the instruction asks for a conditional action based on recency, never return the retrieved date alone; always perform the comparison and take the appropriate branch.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent checked active medications but only looked at MedicationRequest resources. Missed opioid orders that may be in MedicationDispense or other order types. Concluded no opioids present without verifying all possible order sources.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent failed because it only searched MedicationRequest for active opioid medications, but opioid orders may exist in other resources (e.g., MedicationDispense, MedicationAdministration, or as part of a prescription workflow). The agent made a premature negative conclusion without exhaustive search.
action_guidelines: [P1] When verifying active opioid orders, search across all relevant FHIR resources (MedicationRequest, MedicationDispense, MedicationAdministration) to avoid missing orders.
[P1] If no opioids found in one resource type, do not conclude absence until all possible order sources have been checked.
[P1] Document which specific FHIR resources were searched and their results before concluding no opioids exist.

[P1] If required data is missing or insufficient, document which specific criteria are not met and conclude no action.

[P1] When searching for procedures in FHIR, always use standard coding systems (CPT, SNOMED, LOINC) rather than local or internal codes. If initial search fails, try alternative standard code systems or search by procedure name before concluding.
[P1] When paginating through results, first verify that the current results contain the target observation type; if not, stop and diagnose the query parameters.
[P1] For active medication orders, use MedicationRequest resource with status=active and include all relevant opioid codes.
[P1] When searching for medications, use RxNorm codes via the 'code' parameter, not free-text names.
[P1] When checking vaccination status, always look for the most recent vaccine date first.

[P1] Before querying orders or medications, verify patient existence via Patient resource lookup; if patient not found, report error and halt.
[P1] Do not assume missing data implies a need for action; verify all protocol conditions explicitly.
[P1] First count the number of matching active orders, then branch based on count (0, 1, multiple).
[P1] Before ordering a vaccine, always retrieve the patient's immunization history to check the most recent dose date.

[P1] When checking recency of a prior procedure, first search for any occurrence without a time window, then filter by the required window.

[P1] When checking procedure recency, first confirm the exact procedure code matches the required modality (e.g., CT Abdomen without pelvis); if multiple similar codes exist, retrieve the specific one matching the instruction before evaluating date constraints.
[P1] If a search action is rejected, review the parameter syntax and retry with corrected query parameters.
[P1] When ordering a procedure, use the appropriate CPT code for the specific modality and contrast (e.g., CT Abdomen with IV contrast = CPT 74177).
[P1] Before executing a FHIR search, verify the parameter structure against the FHIR specification for that resource type.
